themes: Bills offensive line vs. elite pass-rushers
writeup: June 10, 2026 was a busy day in Bills country as Buffalo wrapped up the second session of their three-day mandatory minicamp under first-year head coach Joe Brady, with the team releasing five roster moves including adding linebacker depth and cutting a wide receiver. The biggest storyline shadowing the on-field work was linebacker Dorian Williams, who was spotted on crutches and unable to participate, with Brady confirming an injury but declining to specify its nature — a peculiar level of secrecy for a spring practice. On the brighter side, pass rusher Michael Hoecht continued to inspire optimism by participating in individual drills as he works his way back from a season-ending Achilles tear, while star running back James Cook showed up and addressed his OTA absence, citing time with family in Miami. Meanwhile, analysts spotlighted a daunting 2026 schedule challenge: Buffalo's offensive line is set to face seven of the top ten sack leaders from last season, a gauntlet made even tougher by Myles Garrett's trade to the Rams.
